description_long: This is a commercial passenger airplane painted in the Lufthansa livery, featuring a streamlined fuselage with a white body and a blue tail fin adorned with the yellow Lufthansa logo. The aircraft has two large turbofan engines mounted under its low-set, swept-back wings, which are designed for efficient high-speed flight. The cockpit is positioned at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the flight crew. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers in a conventional arrangement. The landing gear is tricycle-type, with visible main gear under the wings and a nose gear beneath the cockpit. The airplane's exterior is primarily smooth and metallic, with clear demarcations for doors, windows, and control surfaces.
description: A Lufthansa-branded commercial passenger airplane with low, swept-back wings, twin underwing turbofan engines, and a blue tail fin featuring the airline's logo. The fuselage is white and streamlined, with a forward cockpit and tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full Lufthansa branding, blue tail fin with logo, and both engines under the wings.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail section, horizontal stabilizers, and the symmetry of the wings and engines.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the fuselage, engines, and tail fin, mirroring view 0 but from the other side.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the nose, cockpit windows, and the forward placement of the engines under the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back configuration with no visible winglets and moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear unit
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows
